mnemonics.title: BST Search Pattern
mnemonics.content: Left is Less, Right is Greater - always compare and choose your direction wisely, like choosing left for smaller adventure!

mnemonics.title: Inorder Successor Finding
mnemonics.content: If Right subtree exists, go Right then All-the-Way LEFT. If no Right, go Up until Parent-was-Left (P-W-A-L).

acronyms.usage: Fundamental data structure for efficient searching, insertion, and deletion operations
Self-balancing BST where heights of left and right subtrees differ by at most 1
Memory cleanup order - process children before parent

visualTips.visualization: Think of BST as organized bookshelf: books to left are smaller titles (A-M), books to right are larger titles (N-Z). Finding a book means checking which section and narrowing down.
Balanced BST looks like triangle (wide at bottom). Skewed BST looks like a diagonal line - one branch only, like a vine growing one direction.
Imagine walking along the tree: always take the left path first, visit the node, then take right path. You naturally visit nodes in sorted order like walking through houses on a street in order.

analogies.analogy: Looking for a word in a dictionary using the knowledge that words before are in left pages and after in right pages - you keep dividing the search space in half.
Finding a parking spot in a row where cars are sorted by size: you find the correct empty spot maintaining the sorted order, similar to inserting at leaf position.
A skewed BST is like a one-lane road where you must pass through every car to reach the end - no shortcuts exist, unlike a balanced tree where multiple lanes allow faster access.
